jokes.text: I was thinking about the wig historically. I guess The Revolutionary War was the last war fought in wigs...
jokes.setup: Jerry opens with a random observation about wigs in history
jokes.punchline: The absurd idea that an entire war was defined by wig-wearing
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry observational humor with a unique historical angle that sets up the wig theme

jokes.text: They're going into battle; 'Well, do I have my bullets? Do I have my bobby pins?'
jokes.setup: Soldiers preparing for battle
jokes.punchline: Bobby pins equated with bullets as essential war supplies
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of deadly weapons with feminine accessories, very economical

jokes.text: 'What if I get killed? Is my wig on straight?'
jokes.setup: A soldier's dying concern
jokes.punchline: Vanity about appearance even in the face of death
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the wig absurdity but predictable follow-up

jokes.text: I know when it went out; When the mirror was invented.
jokes.setup: When did men's wigs go out of style?
jokes.punchline: Once men could see how ridiculous they looked
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline with brilliant logic and maximum economy

jokes.text: No guy is looking in a mirror... powdering a thatch of horsehair on his head, going; 'All right, let's go rap to the ladies.'
jokes.setup: The mirror revelation
jokes.punchline: The absurd visual of a man preparing to seduce women with horsehair
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy with specific details, though 'rap to the ladies' feels dated

jokes.text: Then they'll match my sneakers.
jokes.setup: Salesman describing the finest antelope horn buttons
jokes.punchline: George's completely tone-deaf response about sneakers
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - missing the point entirely with innocent cluelessness

jokes.text: So he knew you were making it up? Yeah, he caught me. So here's what I want you to do...
jokes.setup: George admits to being caught in a lie
jokes.punchline: His solution is to get Jerry to participate in making the lie true
jokes.explanation: Classic George neurotic logic but the setup is too long

jokes.text: But you were. But if you go back with me, then I'm not.
jokes.setup: Jerry points out George was lying
jokes.punchline: George's twisted logic about retroactively not lying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant George logic - perfectly structured and economical, quintessentially George

jokes.text: Well, Bob Sacamano, he stayed with me once for a year and a half.
jokes.setup: George complaining about a two-week houseguest
jokes.punchline: Kramer's casual mention of an 18-month houseguest
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer absurdity and introduces Bob Sacamano, perfect character response

jokes.text: Who is he? He's a wig master.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about Bob Sacamano
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and absurd job title
jokes.explanation: Connects back to opening wig theme, absurd job title

jokes.text: Boy, imagine liking wigs to the point it becomes a career choice.
jokes.setup: Learning about the wig master profession
jokes.punchline: Jerry's incredulity at professional wig obsession
jokes.explanation: Predictable observational joke but connects themes

jokes.text: You get this cool T-shirt when you sign up. Oh, I'm down.
jokes.setup: Kramer excited about a parking lot deal
jokes.punchline: Getting irrationally excited about a promotional T-shirt
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer character moment but fairly predictable enthusiasm

jokes.text: Remember me? I said I'd come back with someone, and I did. Surprised?
jokes.setup: George returns to the store as promised
jokes.punchline: His smug satisfaction at proving he wasn't lying
jokes.explanation: Good callback to setup, very George to be proud of not lying this time

jokes.text: No, I believed you.
jokes.setup: George's triumphant return
jokes.punchline: Craig's casual response deflating George's moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of George's neurotic triumph, very economical

jokes.text: Those fanciful narratives really take me away.
jokes.setup: Craig praising J. Peterman catalogs
jokes.punchline: Overly flowery language that mimics the catalogs themselves
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of pretentious catalog language but not particularly sharp

jokes.text: You know what? I write those. No.
jokes.setup: Craig praising the catalog copy
jokes.punchline: His disbelief when meeting the actual writer
jokes.explanation: Simple coincidence humor but lacks punch

jokes.text: Well, I love it. You what? I think it's great. I think you should get it.
jokes.setup: George brought Elaine to discourage the purchase
jokes.punchline: She enthusiastically endorses it, forcing him to buy
jokes.explanation: Good plan backfiring comedy, George's scheme unravels

jokes.text: Perhaps you could do the same.
jokes.setup: George writing his phone number on the check
jokes.punchline: Craig smoothly asking Elaine for her number
jokes.explanation: Smooth operator move but predictable romantic comedy beat

jokes.text: You weren't supposed to say that. But I really did like it. That's not the point.
jokes.setup: George's plan completely backfired
jokes.punchline: His frustration at Elaine's honesty ruining his scheme
jokes.explanation: George's neurotic frustration when reality interferes with his plans

jokes.text: What about that guy asking you out right in front of me? What is the big deal?
jokes.setup: George complaining about Craig
jokes.punchline: Elaine's complete indifference to George's emasculation
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic but fairly standard relationship comedy

jokes.text: It was very emasculating. He doesn't know the nature of our relationship. You're there approving new clothes. That's a girlfriend job.
jokes.setup: George explaining why he's upset
jokes.punchline: His specific categorization of 'girlfriend jobs'
jokes.explanation: Good George neurosis about social roles and appearances

jokes.text: How dare he? He dared.
jokes.setup: Elaine's sarcastic 'How dare he?'
jokes.punchline: George's literal, deflated response
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure, maximum economy, George's deflation

jokes.text: I already asked him that. He said, 'Good. Good day.'
jokes.setup: Charmaine asking George about his day
jokes.punchline: Susan's passive-aggressive repetition of the exact exchange
jokes.explanation: Good domestic tension humor, Susan's territorial behavior

jokes.text: Thank God you're home. I'm locked out.
jokes.setup: Kramer arriving at Jerry's
jokes.punchline: Immediately making his problem Jerry's problem
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer making his problems everyone else's but predictable

jokes.text: I didn't tell you to park there. Now someone's gotta drive you every time you need your car? Take the bus. I'm not gonna take the bus. That's why I got a car.
jokes.setup: Jerry pointing out the parking lot's inconvenience
jokes.punchline: Kramer's circular logic about why he won't take the bus
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer logic and city parking frustrations but not exceptional

jokes.text: Forget it. I'm gonna get George to pick me up. He won't take you. Got it, got it. Hello? Can you take me over to Jiffy Park? Yeah, I'll pick you up right now.
jokes.setup: Jerry predicting George won't help
jokes.punchline: George immediately agreeing to help, proving Jerry wrong
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expectations, George's helpful nature contradicts Jerry's prediction

jokes.text: Would you pick a station! I like them all.
jokes.setup: Susan's frustration with the houseguest
jokes.punchline: Charmaine's oblivious response revealing her indecisiveness
jokes.explanation: Standard houseguest annoyance humor but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Oh, great. Now the volume knob fell off.
jokes.setup: Radio problems with the houseguest
jokes.punchline: Things getting progressively worse
jokes.explanation: Weak escalation, too predictable and not specific enough

jokes.text: What's this? Oh, God. What? It's a c... It's a condom!
jokes.setup: George finding something in the car
jokes.punchline: The awkward discovery in front of everyone
jokes.explanation: Standard embarrassment comedy but the hesitation in delivery adds to the awkwardness

jokes.text: Except for Jerry.
jokes.setup: Craig talking about upscale clientele
jokes.punchline: Casually dismissing Jerry as not upscale
jokes.explanation: Good casual insult delivery, very economical

jokes.text: But I barely know you. Well, we'll just have to do something about that, won't we?
jokes.setup: Elaine's hesitation about accepting the dress offer
jokes.punchline: Craig's smooth, slightly predatory response
jokes.explanation: Generic smooth-talking guy dialogue, lacks originality

jokes.text: Hey, Greg. It's Craig.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to be casual with Craig
jokes.punchline: Getting his name wrong immediately
jokes.explanation: Standard name-forgetting comedy but economical delivery

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, we went way out and wild.
jokes.setup: Jerry claiming he and Elaine dated
jokes.punchline: Exaggerating their relationship with awkward phrasing
jokes.explanation: Jerry's attempt to sound casual about their past relationship comes off awkward

jokes.text: How do you know she's not my wife?
jokes.setup: Rose seller assuming Elaine isn't Jerry's wife
jokes.punchline: Jerry's indignation, mirroring George's earlier complaint
jokes.explanation: Good callback to George's earlier complaint, Jerry experiencing the same thing

jokes.text: Hey, look, you walk in this city, things are gonna stick to your foot. You open your car and... condom.
jokes.setup: George asking about the condom in his car
jokes.punchline: Kramer's matter-of-fact explanation of city life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer logic about city life, great observational humor with his casual delivery

jokes.text: I'm terribly sorry, Mr. Kramer... but we can't get your car now. The keys have been misplaced.
jokes.setup: Kramer needing his car
jokes.punchline: The parking lot losing his keys, making the situation worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the parking lot problems but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Take that pink Cadillac Eldorado over there. It's a Mary Kay car.
jokes.setup: Attendant offering Kramer a replacement car
jokes.punchline: It's a distinctive pink Mary Kay cosmetics car
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup - Kramer driving a bright pink car would be inherently funny

jokes.text: Something funny going on here.
jokes.setup: George deciding to stay at the parking lot
jokes.punchline: His suspicion about the lot's operations
jokes.explanation: Sets up George's investigation but not inherently funny on its own

jokes.text: Well, what about the ponytail? What about it? Come on. Ponytail? Get real.
jokes.setup: Jerry pointing out Craig's ponytail
jokes.punchline: His implication that ponytails are automatically disqualifying
jokes.explanation: Jerry's shallow male prejudice about hairstyles, somewhat dated humor

jokes.text: this guy's gonna dangle that dress in front of you... like a dirt farmer dangles a carrot in front of a mule.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining Craig's strategy
jokes.punchline: The specific and absurd farming metaphor
jokes.explanation: Great specific metaphor that's both visual and absurd, good Jerry observational style

jokes.text: Like a shark fisherman with chum.
jokes.setup: Jerry's first metaphor about dangling the dress
jokes.punchline: Escalating with another predator/bait metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but slightly weaker than the first metaphor

jokes.text: Like a shrimp farmer... Okay.
jokes.setup: Jerry continuing his metaphor series
jokes.punchline: Elaine cutting him off before he can finish the third one
jokes.explanation: Good comedic interruption, Elaine stopping Jerry's rambling metaphors

jokes.text: Behold, the Technicolor Dreamcoat.
jokes.setup: Bob showing off the theatrical costume
jokes.punchline: His dramatic presentation of what's essentially a work uniform
jokes.explanation: Connects to the wig master storyline but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: They stink.
jokes.setup: George mentioning he works for the Yankees
jokes.punchline: The prostitute's matter-of-fact dismissal of the team
jokes.explanation: Good unexpected response from an unexpected source, very economical

jokes.text: Turning tricks?
jokes.setup: George trying to use street terminology
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to sound knowledgeable about prostitution
jokes.explanation: Good George awkwardness trying to be street-smart

jokes.text: You have change for 20? Fifteen? Well....
jokes.setup: George negotiating with a prostitute for information
jokes.punchline: The awkward price negotiation for what he thinks is just talking
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with George's naivety about what he's actually negotiating for

jokes.text: George? Hi, honey.
jokes.setup: George in compromising position with prostitute
jokes.punchline: Susan arriving at the worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Classic bad timing comedy, very economical setup and payoff

jokes.text: Even a tiny doubt, a dot of a doubt... Because if there's any doubt at all, I feel that we should cultivate it. Cultivate it?
jokes.setup: George talking about trust in their relationship
jokes.punchline: His suggestion that they should actively nurture any doubts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant George neurosis - the idea of cultivating doubt is perfectly backwards and character-specific

jokes.text: But you said it was in the store. No, no, no. We sold out. We've had to order some more. But I thought Nicole Miller was ma... Well...
jokes.setup: Elaine questioning Craig's story about the dress
jokes.punchline: Craig's increasingly elaborate lies falling apart
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Craig being exposed as a fraud but not particularly sharp

jokes.text: So you getting him a discount too? Well, why so surprised?
jokes.setup: Elaine realizing Craig offers discounts to everyone
jokes.punchline: Craig's casual admission that he's not special
jokes.explanation: Elaine's realization that she's not special to Craig, decent revelation moment

jokes.text: You know what I'm gonna do? I'm gonna take that jacket back. I'm putting this guy right out of commission.
jokes.setup: Jerry's frustration with Craig
jokes.punchline: His petty plan for revenge through returns
jokes.explanation: Jerry's petty revenge plan but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Turn in? Yeah, I had a tough day. It's only 9:00. Yeah, well, you know, I don't argue with the body, Jerry. That's an argument you can't win.
jokes.setup: Kramer wanting to sleep at 9 PM
jokes.punchline: His pseudo-philosophical justification
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer pseudo-wisdom about listening to his body

jokes.text: Hey, look, you know, you're the one who's locked out. I'm letting you stay here. You're wearing my bathrobe. You should adapt to me.
jokes.setup: Jerry complaining about the early bedtime
jokes.punchline: Kramer's completely backwards logic about who should accommodate whom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer entitlement and backwards logic as a houseguest

jokes.text: Well, can I sleep with you?
jokes.setup: Kramer complaining about the couch
jokes.punchline: His casual suggestion to share Jerry's bed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer boundary crossing, delivered with complete innocence

jokes.text: Why? Do I really have to explain why?
jokes.setup: Kramer asking why he can't sleep with Jerry
jokes.punchline: Jerry's incredulous response
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry straight-man reaction to Kramer's obliviousness

jokes.text: So I am positive you are wrong about Craig. Yeah, why? Because he told a man he'd give him a discount too. A man, Jerry.
jokes.setup: Elaine defending Craig against Jerry's accusations
jokes.punchline: Her proof based on Craig's equal-opportunity discount offering
jokes.explanation: Elaine's flawed logic about Craig's motivations but not exceptional

jokes.text: Well, perhaps he thinks that you're working him for the discount. What do I mean? Well, perhaps he thinks that you're working him for the discount. Shaking that little butt of yours into big, big savings.
jokes.setup: Jerry suggesting Craig might think Elaine is using him
jokes.punchline: The specific and crude description of Elaine's supposed strategy
jokes.explanation: Jerry's crass but specific description of transactional dating

jokes.text: The cornerstones of a healthy relationship.
jokes.setup: Jerry talking about sex and discounts
jokes.punchline: Calling them relationship cornerstones with mock seriousness
jokes.explanation: Good cynical observation about modern relationships with mock gravitas

jokes.text: This patio furniture's on sale.
jokes.setup: Kramer in bed with Jerry
jokes.punchline: His casual comment about furniture while in this awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer non-sequitur in an already awkward situation

jokes.text: Ideally.
jokes.setup: George saying people should be able to get their cars from a parking lot
jokes.punchline: The attendant's casual 'ideally' as if it's not a basic expectation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that makes the basic service sound like an unreasonable expectation

jokes.text: For spite.
jokes.setup: Store clerk asking why Jerry wants to return the jacket
jokes.punchline: Jerry's brutally honest and unusual reason
jokes.explanation: Brilliant honesty and economy - spite as a return reason is perfectly Jerry and unexpectedly honest

jokes.text: I don't think you can return an item for spite.
jokes.setup: Jerry's spite return request
jokes.punchline: The clerk treating spite as an invalid return category
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to Jerry's spite line, absurd customer service logic

jokes.text: Well, you already said spite, so.... But I changed my mind. No, you said spite. Too late.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to change his return reason
jokes.punchline: The manager's rigid adherence to Jerry's first stated reason
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the spite logic, absurd bureaucratic inflexibility

jokes.text: Nice hat. Get a calendar, honey. It's the '90s.
jokes.setup: Employee commenting on Jerry's hat
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic response about fashion being current
jokes.explanation: Decent comeback but feels dated and not particularly clever

jokes.text: Ever thought of selling it? It would make a brilliant wig.
jokes.setup: Charmaine complimenting Elaine's hair
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggesting she sell it for wig-making
jokes.explanation: Good callback to wig theme and shows Charmaine's practical theater mindset

jokes.text: There's your champagne coolie. Well, looks like it's just you and me, cowboy.
jokes.setup: Server bringing drinks to Elaine and Jerry
jokes.punchline: Elaine's unexpectedly forward come-on to Jerry
jokes.explanation: Elaine being unexpectedly aggressive but not particularly funny

jokes.text: Well, the funniest thing. I've learned that the new shipment's coming by boat... which does tend to take a bit longer, what with the waves and all.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking about the Nicole Miller dresses
jokes.punchline: Craig's elaborate lie about shipping delays with condescending explanation
jokes.explanation: Good Craig smarmy character moment with condescending 'what with the waves and all'

jokes.text: Yeah, I guess you could say that. Right in front of me? How do you know we're not together?
jokes.setup: Jesse asking Ethan out in front of Jerry
jokes.punchline: Jerry experiencing the same emasculation as George earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback - Jerry experiencing exactly what George complained about earlier

jokes.text: Well, it's very emasculating.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining his objection
jokes.punchline: Using George's exact word from earlier
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback using George's exact terminology, showing Jerry understands now

jokes.text: Nicole Millers? We aren't expecting any Nicole Millers. In fact, we have too many as it is.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about Nicole Miller dresses
jokes.punchline: Complete contradiction of Craig's lies
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that exposes Craig's entire scheme

jokes.text: Hey, you just cost me some money. Hey, cool it, lady. Cool it. Cool it, lady. Cool it.
jokes.setup: Prostitute angry at Kramer for interrupting business
jokes.punchline: Kramer's repeated, ineffective attempt to calm her down
jokes.explanation: Standard Kramer in over his head but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Okay, big daddy, take the hat off.
jokes.setup: Police arresting Kramer
jokes.punchline: Officer's assumption about Kramer's role based on appearance
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy from Kramer's theatrical costume making him look like a pimp

jokes.text: All right, turn to your right. I said turn, pimp!
jokes.setup: Officer processing Kramer's arrest
jokes.punchline: Getting increasingly frustrated with what he sees as defiance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the mistaken identity but fairly predictable

jokes.text: I'm not a pimp.
jokes.setup: Police treating Kramer like a pimp
jokes.punchline: Kramer's simple, matter-of-fact denial
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer innocent protest but somewhat predictable ending
